        "* **Loaded** for inference without retraining\n",
        "* **Deployed** in a Streamlit web application or REST API\n",
        "* **Shared** via GitHub for full reproducibility\n"
      ]

            "  Building     Prediction              Claim Probability\n",
            "──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
            "  Building 1    ✓  LOW RISK             31.1%  [██████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░]\n",
            "  Building 2    ✓  LOW RISK             14.5%  [██░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░]\n",
            "  Building 3    ✓  LOW RISK             17.6%  [███░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░]\n",
            "  Building 4    ✓  LOW RISK             14.9%  [██░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░]\n",
            "  Building 5    ✓  LOW RISK             15.2%  [███░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░]\n",
            "──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
            "\n",
            "✓ Deployment inference demo complete — pipeline confirmed working.\n"
          ]
        }
      ],
      "source": [
        "# ── Load the saved model and run a deployment inference demo ─────────────────\n",
        "loaded_model = joblib.load('best_random_forest_model.joblib')\n",
        "print(\"✓ Model loaded successfully from disk.\")\n",
        "\n",
        "# ── Run inference on 5 sample buildings from the test set ─────────────────────\n",
        "sample_data        = X_test.iloc[:5]\n",
        "sample_predictions = loaded_model.predict(sample_data)\n",
        "sample_proba       = loaded_model.predict_proba(sample_data)[:, 1]\n",
        "\n",
        "print(\"\\nSample Inference — Claim Risk Assessment:\")\n",
        "print('─' * 62)\n",
        "print(f\"  {'Building':<12} {'Prediction':<22} {'Claim Probability':>18}\")\n",
        "print('─' * 62)\n",
        "for i, (pred, prob) in enumerate(zip(sample_predictions, sample_proba)):\n",
        "    status   = \"⚠️  CLAIM RISK\" if pred == 1 else \"✓  LOW RISK\"\n",
        "    risk_bar = '█' * int(prob * 20) + '░' * (20 - int(prob * 20))\n",
        "    print(f\"  Building {i+1:<3}  {status:<22} {prob:>6.1%}  [{risk_bar}]\")\n",
        "print('─' * 62)\n",
        "print(\"\\n✓ Deployment inference demo complete — pipeline confirmed working.\")"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "fCj4TpuJ2JCC"
      },
      "source": [
        "---\n",
        "# STEP 6: BUSINESS INSIGHTS AND RECOMMENDATIONS\n",
        "\n",
        "The technical work is complete. This final step translates every finding — from data cleaning through SHAP analysis — into **actionable intelligence for the insurer**.\n",
        "\n",
        "As Lead Data Analyst, the deliverable is not just a model. It is a framework for smarter, more data-driven underwriting decisions.\n"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "ildSkuJq2JCC"
      },
      "source": [
        "## 1. Key Findings from the Data\n",
        "\n",
        "**A. The Claims Problem is Real but a Minority Event**\n",
        "* **1,626 of 7,014 buildings (23.18%)** filed at least one claim — a 3.3:1 imbalance.\n",
        "* A naïve model that always predicts \"No Claim\" achieves 76.82% accuracy by default — confirming why accuracy alone is a misleading success metric for this problem.\n",
        "\n",
        "**B. Building Dimension is the Dominant Risk Signal**\n",
        "* `log_building_dimension` + `building_dimension` account for **42.95%** of total feature importance.\n",
        "* Claim buildings have a **median dimension of 1,995.5 m²** vs **900.0 m²** for non-claim buildings — a 2.2× difference in median size.\n",
        "* SHAP confirms this is a near-monotonic relationship: larger building → higher claim probability.\n",
        "\n",
        "**C. Building Age is the Second Most Important Factor**\n",
        "* `log_building_age` + `building_age` account for a combined **25.78%** of feature importance.\n",
        "* Average building age: 49.1 years. Range: 0 to 316 years.\n",
        "* Older buildings claim more frequently — reflecting accumulated wear, outdated materials, and deferred maintenance.\n",
        "\n",
        "**D. Number of Windows Amplifies the Risk Signal**\n",
        "* `number_of_windows` contributes **8.22%** of feature importance — the only other feature above 8%.\n",
        "* Claim buildings have a **median of 3.0 windows** vs **1.0 window** for non-claim buildings — a 3× difference.\n",
        "\n",
        "**E. The Urban/Rural Dimension is a Consistent but Modest Signal**\n",
        "* `building_fenced`, `garden`, and `settlement` each show a **3.8 percentage point** claim rate differential (25.0% rural vs 21.2% urban).\n",
        "* These three features are near-perfectly correlated — together encoding one underlying risk dimension, not three independent signals.\n",
        "\n",
        "**F. Building Type Shows a Clear Risk Gradient**\n",
        "* Building Type contributes **4.96%** of feature importance.\n",
        "* Type 4 buildings have the highest claim rate; Type 1 the lowest — a consistent ordering across all analysis steps.\n"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "9GFP8N982JCD"
      },
      "source": [
        "## 2. Model Recommendation\n",
        "\n",
        "After evaluating three classification algorithms, tuning the best model using GridSearchCV (216 combinations, 1,080 model fits), and validating with 5-fold cross-validation, the **tuned Random Forest Classifier** is the recommended model for deployment.\n",
        "\n",
        "**Best hyperparameters (from GridSearchCV):**\n",
        "* `max_depth`: 20\n",
        "* `max_features`: sqrt\n",
        "* `min_samples_leaf`: 1\n",
        "* `min_samples_split`: 2\n",
        "* `n_estimators`: 200\n",
        "\n",
        "**Performance summary:**\n",
        "\n",
        "| Criterion | Logistic Regression | Decision Tree | **Tuned Random Forest** |\n",
        "|-----------|--------------------|--------------|-----------------------|\n",
        "| Accuracy | 0.6636 | 0.6536 | **0.7106** |\n",
        "| Precision | 0.3527 | 0.2952 | **0.3608** |\n",
        "| Recall | **0.5415** | 0.3569 | 0.3231 |\n",
        "| F1-Score | **0.4272** | 0.3231 | 0.3409 |\n",
        "| ROC-AUC | **0.6629** | 0.5498 | 0.6144 |\n",
        "| CV F1-Score | — | — | **0.7921** |\n",
        "\n",
        "The tuned Random Forest is recommended on the basis of highest accuracy, highest precision, and the most stable cross-validated performance. Logistic Regression may be preferred operationally if catching claims is more important than reducing false positives.\n",
        "\n",
        "> **Caveat:** All models show room for improvement in Recall. The model should be re-evaluated every 12 months as new claims data becomes available.\n"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "t-XoWAun2JCD"
      },
      "source": [
        "## 3. Actionable Business Recommendations\n",
        "\n",
        "### Recommendation 1: Risk-Based Premium Pricing\n",
        "Use `building_dimension`, `building_age`, and `number_of_windows` as **primary rating factors** in the premium engine.\n",
        "* Large, old buildings with many windows attract materially higher claim rates and should carry correspondingly higher premiums.\n",
        "* Aligns price with actual risk — reducing adverse selection (high-risk customers getting underpriced coverage).\n",
        "\n",
        "### Recommendation 2: Tiered Risk Classification by Building Type\n",
        "Immediately segment the portfolio by `building_type`:\n",
        "* **Type 4 and Type 3**: High-risk tier — apply stricter underwriting criteria, higher premiums, or mandatory pre-inspection.\n",
        "* **Type 1**: Low-risk tier — could qualify for preferential pricing to attract more low-risk business.\n",
        "\n",
        "### Recommendation 3: Targeted Pre-Renewal Inspections\n",
        "Deploy the saved model as a **risk scoring engine** before policy renewal:\n",
        "* Buildings scoring a claim probability > 60% are automatically flagged for physical inspection before renewal acceptance.\n",
        "* Focuses inspection resources on the highest-risk segment — maximising risk detection while minimising cost.\n",
        "\n",
        "### Recommendation 4: Residential Portfolio Review\n",
        "Residential buildings claim at a higher rate than their share of the portfolio suggests.\n",
        "* Conduct a dedicated actuarial review of residential policy pricing.\n",
        "* Consider building a **separate residential risk model** as the portfolio grows.\n",
        "\n",
        "### Recommendation 5: Maintenance Incentive Programme\n",
        "`garden`, `building_fenced`, and `building_painted` all correlate with claim rates — likely proxying **property maintenance culture**:\n",
        "* Offer premium discounts to policyholders who provide evidence of regular maintenance (e.g., inspection certificates at renewal).\n",
        "* Incentivises behaviour that reduces insurer liability — a win-win for both parties.\n",
        "\n",
        "### Recommendation 6: Early Warning Scoring at New Business Application\n",
        "Integrate the model into the **new business application workflow**:\n",
        "* Applicants with a predicted claim probability above a defined threshold are automatically referred for underwriter review before policy issuance.\n",
        "* Reduces the risk of onboarding high-risk buildings at standard premiums.\n"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "Sdd5c3LK2JCE"
      },
      "source": [
        "## 4. Limitations and Next Steps\n",
        "\n",
        "### Current Limitations\n",
        "\n",
        "| Limitation | Impact | Recommended Mitigation |\n",
        "|-----------|--------|------------------------|\n",
        "| **Geographic granularity lost** | `geo_code` was dropped — location risk (flood zones, fire station proximity) unaccounted for | Enrich with geocoded risk scores |\n",
        "| **Claims severity not modelled** | We predict *whether* a claim occurs, not *how costly* it will be | Build a companion regression model for claim amount |\n",
        "| **Temporal drift** | Dataset covers 2012–2016 only — patterns may have shifted | Annual model retraining pipeline |\n",
        "| **`building_age` anomaly** | The 1545 occupancy year creates an extreme outlier — handled via log transformation | Validate with data provider; consider capping at a percentile |\n",
        "| **Binary target limitation** | Minor and catastrophic claims both receive `claim = 1` | Multi-class or severity-weighted target variable |\n",
        "\n",
        "### Recommended Next Steps\n",
        "\n",
        "1. **Build a Claim Severity Model:** A regression model trained on claim amount for the subset where `claim = 1` — to complete the full risk picture.\n",
        "\n",
        "2. **Enrich with External Data:** Weather event data, flood zone classifications, proximity to emergency services, and building permit records could significantly improve predictive accuracy.\n",
        "\n",
        "3. **Deploy as a Streamlit Dashboard:** A web application that lets underwriters input building characteristics and receive an instant risk score, SHAP-based explanation, and premium guidance.\n",
        "\n",
        "4. **Establish an Annual Retraining Pipeline:** Schedule model retraining every 12 months with accumulated claims data, monitoring for performance drift using a KS test or Population Stability Index (PSI).\n",
        "\n",
        "5. **Explore Advanced Algorithms:** XGBoost, LightGBM, and CatBoost are likely to outperform Random Forest on this dataset — worth evaluating with proper hyperparameter tuning.\n"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "YpslJMya2JCE"
      },
      "source": [
        "## 5. Project Summary\n",
        "\n",
        "| Phase | Action Taken | Key Output |\n",
        "|-------|-------------|------------|\n",
        "| **Data Loading** | Loaded data dictionary + main dataset via Google Drive | 7,160 raw building records |\n",
        "| **Data Inspection** | Shape, info, describe, unique values inspection | 3 columns with missing values; `number_of_windows` required 3-step cleaning |\n",
        "| **Data Cleaning** | Column standardisation, type correction, mode imputation, deduplication | **7,014 clean records** (146 duplicates removed) |\n",
        "| **Feature Engineering** | Created `building_age` from datetime columns | Age range: 0 to 316 years, Mean: 49.1 years |\n",
        "| **Skewness Analysis** | Quantified skewness across all numerical features | 3 features flagged as Highly Skewed (|skew| ≥ 1.0) |\n",
        "| **Outlier Detection** | IQR method across all numerical features | Outliers retained — handled via log transformation |\n",
        "| **EDA — Univariate** | Target, numerical, and categorical distribution analysis | Class imbalance 3.3:1 confirmed; `building_dimension` top predictor identified |\n",
        "| **EDA — Bivariate** | Correlation, contingency tables, grouped statistics | Triple redundancy of `building_fenced`/`garden`/`settlement` confirmed |\n",
        "| **Feature Transformation** | log1p and reflected log1p applied | `building_dimension` skewness: +3.12 → −0.20 |\n",
        "| **One-Hot Encoding** | 4 categorical columns → 4 binary dummy columns | All 13 features fully numerical |\n",
        "| **Train-Test Split** | 80/20 stratified split | Train: 5,611, Test: 1,403 samples |\n",
        "| **SMOTE** | Applied to training data only (post-split) | Train: 5,611 → **8,620** samples (50/50 balance) |\n",
        "| **StandardScaler** | Fit on training, transform both sets | All 7 numerical features: μ=0, σ=1 |\n",
        "| **Model Training** | Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest | All 3 trained on 8,620 SMOTE-balanced records |\n",
        "| **Model Evaluation** | Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1, ROC-AUC, Confusion Matrix | Random Forest: best Accuracy (0.7106) and Precision (0.3608) |\n",
        "| **Hyperparameter Tuning** | GridSearchCV: 216 combinations, 1,080 fits | Best: `max_depth=20`, `n_estimators=200`, `max_features=sqrt` |\n",
        "| **Cross-Validation** | 5-fold CV on tuned model | Mean F1 = **0.7921** ± 0.1063 |\n",
        "| **Feature Importance** | Gini impurity-based ranking | `log_building_dimension` #1 (21.64%); top 4 features = 68.73% of importance |\n",
        "| **SHAP Interpretability** | TreeExplainer on 1,403 test samples | Directional confirmation: large + old buildings push toward Claim |\n",
        "| **Model Saving** | joblib serialisation | `best_random_forest_model.joblib` — production ready |\n",
        "| **Business Insights** | 6 actionable recommendations | Risk-based pricing, tiered inspections, residential review |\n",
        "\n",
        "---\n",
        "\n",
        "**Final Conclusion:**\n",
        "\n",
        "The tuned Random Forest model — trained on 8,620 SMOTE-balanced records, validated across 5 cross-validation folds (mean F1 = 0.7921), and interpreted using SHAP — provides the insurer with a transparent, data-driven tool for building claim risk assessment.\n",
        "\n",
        "Building dimension (42.95% of importance) and building age (25.78%) are the dominant predictors, with SHAP confirming their directional influence at the individual prediction level. Integrated into the underwriting workflow, this model can meaningfully support premium pricing decisions, targeted pre-renewal inspections, and new business risk screening.\n",
        "\n",
        "*This notebook is portfolio-ready and has been uploaded to GitHub. The saved model file (`best_random_forest_model.joblib`) is available for Streamlit deployment.*\n"
      ]
    }
